titleStmt.title: Transcription and Translation of NRS GD45/13/280

respStmt.resp: Exported from Models of Authority data
respStmt.name: tei_exporter

availability.p: Status unknown. Exported for research and migration workflows.

revisionDesc.change: transcription moa=258 status=live modified=2017-02-27 19:27:39.785882+00 | translation moa=259 status=live modified=2017-02-27 19:27:43.844111+00

p.cl: Willelmus diuina permissione Ecclesie sancti Andr’ Humilis Minister . Omnibus hoc scriptum visuris uel audituris . Eternam in domino Salutem .

Nouerit vniuersitas
uestra nos concessisse ⁊ hac carta nostra confirmasse Conuentui Monialium deo famulancium apud Nordberwic’ donationem quam eis fecit Comes Duncanus .
de fif . per cartam suam de ecclesia de Largav . Concessionem etiam de eadem ecclesia eisdem Moniablibus factam a Comite Malcolmo de fif . per cartam suam .
Concedimus etiam ⁊ confirmamus eidem conuentui prelibatam ecclesiam de Largav . cum omnibus iustis pertinenciis suis habendam in usus proprios suos ad suam ⁊ pau
perum sustentationem salua eiusdem ecclesie vicaria . Magistro Hugoni medico in tota vita sua . quo cedente uel decedente predicte moniales nobis uel suc
cessoribus nostris ad predictam vicariam ydoneum uicarium presentare tenebuntur .

Teste fratre Iacob’ canonico de Edeneburc . Andrea Decano de tining
ham . Magistro Martin’ Medico domini Regis . Geruasio de Neafle . Andr’ de Morauia . Willelmo de Golin’ ⁊ Nich’ clericis nostris . Hug’ capellano vicario
de Nordberwic’ . Petro Capellano nostro . Ric’ . Capellano . Roberto de vpsetlintun’ . Ric’ de Lidel . Ric’ de tyford . Johanne Marcaldo seruientibus nostris .
William, by divine permission humble servant of the church of St Andrews, to all who shall view or hear this document, eternal salvation in the Lord:

You all should know that we have granted, and by this our charter made firm, to the convent of nuns serving God at North Berwick, the donation that Earl Duncan of Fife made to them by his own charter in respect of the church of Largo; the grant also of the same church to the same nuns made by Earl Malcolm of Fife by his own charter; we also grant and confirm to the same convent the church of Largo mentioned before, with all its just pertinents for their own use for their own support and the support of paupers, reserving the vicarage of the same church to Master Hugh the physician for the whole of his life; and in granting or yielding this, the foresaid nuns shall be bound to present to us or our successors for the foresaid vicarage a suitable vicar.

Witness Brother James, canon of Edinburgh, Andrew the dean of Tyninghame, Master Martin the king’s physician, Gervase de Néauflé, Andrew of Moray, William of Gullane and Nicholas, our clerks, Hugh the chaplain, vicar of North Berwick, Peter our chaplain, Richard the chaplain, Robert of Upsettlington, Richard of Liddell, Richard of Twyford, John Marcaldus, our sergeands.
